titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/70

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=379 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:19:00.530859+00 | translation moa=380 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:18:57.298388+00

p.cl:
Omnibus sancte matris Ecclesie filiis tam presentibus quam futuris . Alanus filius . Walteri Dapifer domini Regis
Scott' . Salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse et hac carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie sancte marie de Melros
et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus totam illam terram quam tenent de Ricardo le Waleis . scilicet Baremor et
Godenech . per ipsas diuisas que continentur in carta ipsius Ricardi . quam habent de eadem terram . ita ut quicquid infra
easdem diuisas fuerit habeant et possideant in perpetuum ita libere et quiete et honorifice sicut aliqua elemosina
liberius quiecius et honorificentius in regno Regis . Scott' possidetur . Et ego et heredes mei manutenebimus et wa
rantizabimus illis eandem terram contra omnes homines in perpetuum . saluo seruitio meo de ipso Ricardo . et heredibus eius .

His Testibus . Domino Iocelino Glasg' . episcopo . Ricardo . abbate de Drieburc . Helia nepote episcopi . Waltero clerico episcopi
Ricardo . malebise . helia fratre episcopi . Willelmo passelewe . petro de curri .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church both present and to come, Alan son of Walter, steward of the lord king of the Scots, sends greeting.

Know me to have granted, and by this charter to have confirmed, to God and the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks in that place serving God, the whole land that they hold of Richard Wallace, that is Barmuir and Godenech, by the same boundaries that are contained in the charter of the same Richard, which they have concerning the same land, so that whatever was within the same boundaries they may have and possess for ever as freely and quitely and honourably as any alms is freely, quitely and honourably possessed in the kingdom of the king of the Scots. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to them the same land against all men for ever. Saving my service from Richard himself.

With these witnesses: the lord Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; Richard, abbot of Dryburgh; Elias the bishop's nephew; Walter the bishop's clerk; Richard Malebisse; Elias the bishop's brother; William Passelew; Peter of Corrie.
